pi_items.source: setup_moodle.sh creates course with shortname='BIO101' in Science category (line 323)
Task description (task.json line 5) and verifier (export_result.sh line 31) both specify exact name; case-insensitive matching in verifier
Task description specifies glossary entry concept as 'Spaced Repetition' (task.json line 5); verifier checks for this entry with case-insensitive matching (export_result.sh line 50)
Task description specifies glossary entry concept as 'Active Recall' (task.json line 5); verifier checks for this entry with case-insensitive matching (export_result.sh line 53)
Task description specifies block title as 'Daily Study Tip' (task.json line 6); verifier checks for exact match (export_result.sh line 86)

Moodle built-in block type; task description calls it 'Random glossary entry block'; verifier checks for blockname='glossary_random' (export_result.sh lines 28, 71); confirmed as official Moodle block via MoodleDocs

privileged_info_summary: The task targets Moodle 4.5 stable (released October 7, 2024) deployed on Ubuntu with MariaDB and Apache. The BIO101 course (full name: 'Introduction to Biology') is provisioned during setup in the Science category. The agent must create a Glossary activity named 'Student Study Strategies' with two entries: 'Spaced Repetition' and 'Active Recall', then add a glossary_random block titled 'Daily Study Tip' configured to display random entries from the glossary. All metadata values align exactly with the task description and verifier expectations. The Moodle glossary and glossary_random block are standard built-in features verified across Moodle documentation.
